ADR_0001.content:
**Context.** mattpocock/skills is a proven set of agent workflow skills,
but its durable artifacts (issues, ADRs, CONTEXT.md, PRDs) are markdown
and its local issue backend is ``.scratch/`` markdown files.

**Decision.** patdhlk-skills is a fresh repo that ports concepts
selectively — not a fork, not an overlay. Skill names are kept
(``/to-issues``, ``/to-prd``, ``/triage``, ``/qa``, ``/tdd``,
``/diagnose``; ``/grill-me`` ships unchanged) so the set is a drop-in
replacement: uninstall mattpocock/skills where patdhlk-skills is
installed.

**Consequences.** ✅ Familiar vocabulary, 1:1 workflow mapping.
✅ No upstream coupling. ❌ Installing both plugins collides — the README
must say so.
ADR_0001.title: Fresh repo as drop-in replacement for mattpocock/skills

ADR_0002.content:
**Context.** Markdown artifacts (CONTEXT.md, ``docs/adr/*.md``) are easy
to write but carry no IDs, no links, no schema, no traceability.

**Decision.** All durable engineering artifacts in consumer repos —
issues, requirements, features, architecture decisions, glossary terms,
PRDs — are sphinx-needs directives in RST. Tool-mandated files stay
markdown: SKILL.md (Claude Code requires it), CLAUDE.md, README, and
GitHub issue bodies (platform format).

**Consequences.** ✅ Everything durable is in the needs graph: linked,
validated, queryable. ❌ Reading the corpus requires a needs.json build
(:need:`ADR_0006`).
ADR_0002.title: Durable artifacts are sphinx-needs RST, never markdown

ADR_0003.content:
**Context.** Issues may live on GitHub (public collaboration) or locally
as sphinx-needs directives (solo/spec-driven repos). Skills need a
deterministic way to discover which.

**Decision.** A ``[tool.patdhlk-skills]`` table in the consumer repo's
``ubproject.toml`` declares ``issue_backend = "github" | "sphinx-needs"``,
plus ``spec_dir``, ``builder``, and ``issue_doc``. Machine-readable, no
markdown, next to the sphinx-needs config it parameterizes.

**Consequences.** ✅ One lookup, no inference, no ambiguity. ❌ Requires a
one-time setup step per repo (``/setup-patdhlk-skills``).
ADR_0003.title: Issue backend configurable via ubproject.toml

ADR_0004.content:
**Context.** Need-type catalogs are heavily user-dependent — taktora has
17 types, the starter has 3. Skills cannot assume ``.. req::`` or
``.. arch-decision::`` exist.

**Decision.** Setup reads the existing ``ubproject.toml``, proposes a
mapping interactively, and persists it under
``[tool.patdhlk-skills.roles]`` (issue / feature / requirement /
decision / term / test → directive name). Skills only ever look up
roles. Setup never imposes a catalog; it only adds an issue type if the
backend is local and none is mapped.

**Consequences.** ✅ Deterministic at runtime, adapts to any catalog.
❌ A missing role needs one interactive question, then is persisted.
ADR_0004.title: Explicit role map instead of hardcoded directive names

ADR_0005.content:
**Context.** Matt's triage flow uses five GitHub labels; sphinx-needs has
a first-class ``status`` field and schema validation.

**Decision.** The ``:status:`` field carries the triage state directly:
``needs-triage → needs-info | ready-for-agent | ready-for-human →
in-progress → done | wontfix``, enforced via schemas.json. ``:kind:``
classifies (bug / feature / improvement / chore / question). Status edits
happen in place; git history is the audit trail — no separate changelog
field. On the github backend the same state names are labels.

**Consequences.** ✅ One field, one source of truth, filterable in
needtables, schema-enforced transitions at build time. ❌ Local and
GitHub state vocabulary must be kept in sync by the skills.
ADR_0005.title: Issue status carries the triage state machine

ADR_0006.content:
**Context.** Skills must query the corpus ("all needs-triage issues",
"max ID per prefix"). Grepping RST reimplements sphinx-needs parsing and
misses needextend/includes; a full Sphinx build is slow.

**Decision.** Skills build needs.json and query it with jq. The
useblocks ``ubc`` CLI (``ubc build needs``) is preferred — it is faster
than ``sphinx-build``; ``uv run sphinx-build -b needs`` is the fallback.
The choice is persisted as ``builder`` in ``[tool.patdhlk-skills]``.
Skills rebuild before every query rather than caching — stale reads are
worse than slow reads.

**Consequences.** ✅ Canonical data: links resolved, no parsing
fragility. ✅ ubc keeps queries fast. ❌ ubc is a proprietary binary,
fetched per-machine (devcontainer installs it on create).
ADR_0006.title: needs.json is the read path, ubc the preferred builder

ADR_0007.content:
**Context.** Schema rules (:need:`ADR_0005`), link integrity, and
duplicate IDs are only checked at build time.

**Decision.** Every skill mutation of the spec ends with a strict build
(``uv run sphinx-build -W -b html``). A failed gate means the mutation is
fixed before the skill reports success. CI runs the same gate on PRs.

**Consequences.** ✅ The corpus is never left invalid. ✅ Duplicate IDs
from parallel branches surface at merge. ❌ Seconds of build time per
mutation.
ADR_0007.title: Strict build gate after every mutation

ADR_0008.content:
**Context.** New needs require unique IDs; allocation must be derivable
from the corpus without a coordination service.

**Decision.** Take the highest numeric suffix for the prefix from a
fresh needs.json build and add one. No gaps, no hashing. Collisions from
parallel branches are caught by the strict gate (:need:`ADR_0007`) as
duplicate-ID build errors and renumbered at merge time.

**Consequences.** ✅ Simplest possible scheme, small IDs. ❌ No room for
manual inserts between siblings; ordering carries no meaning.
ADR_0008.title: Dense max+1 ID allocation

ADR_0009.content:
**Context.** With ``issue_backend = "github"``, requirements and ADRs
still live as sphinx-needs directives — traceability must cross the
GitHub/spec seam.

**Decision.** GitHub issue bodies carry the need IDs they implement
(greppable ``Implements: REQ_0500, FEAT_0051`` line); the need side
carries a ``:github:`` extra field with the issue number. Skills maintain
both ends on create/close. No build-time enforcement across the seam.

**Consequences.** ✅ Both directions queryable (jq on needs.json, gh
search on bodies). ❌ Convention, not enforced — drift is possible and
only skill discipline prevents it.
ADR_0009.title: Two-way GitHub traceability by convention

ADR_0010.content:
**Context.** Matt's ``/to-prd`` publishes a frozen markdown memo to the
issue tracker. In a needs graph a PRD decomposes naturally.

**Decision.** ``/to-prd`` produces one RST document per PRD: a single
``feat`` directive (motivation, scope, non-goals as prose) plus child
``req`` directives linked ``:satisfies:`` the feature. ``/to-issues``
then slices reqs into issues linked back to the req IDs.

**Consequences.** ✅ The PRD is a living spec inside the graph, not a
frozen memo. ❌ No single "PRD artifact" with its own lifecycle.
ADR_0010.title: A PRD is a feat directive plus child reqs

ADR_0011.content:
**Context.** The pharaoh plugin already drafts, reviews, and ID-allocates
sphinx-needs artefacts, with heavy ASPICE/ISO-26262 process assumptions
(``.pharaoh/`` project dir, workflows.yaml).

**Decision.** patdhlk-skills implements its own lightweight RST writing
and needs.json reading inline. No dependency in either direction.
Conventions are kept compatible — same link types (satisfies / refines /
implements / verifies) and ID style — so both suites can coexist on one
repo.

**Consequences.** ✅ Installable by anyone without pharaoh; lean repos
stay lean. ❌ Some duplicated drafting logic between the two suites.
ADR_0011.title: Standalone from pharaoh — shared conventions only

ADR_0012.content:
**Context.** The flagship repo should exercise its own local backend.

**Decision.** This repo carries its own ``spec/`` (issues, ADRs, reqs as
directives), its own ``[tool.patdhlk-skills]`` with
``issue_backend = "sphinx-needs"``, and the sphinx-needs-starter
devcontainer (Python 3.12 + uv + graphviz/plantuml image, ubc fetched on
create, ``uv sync`` post-create). The founding interview is recorded as
these ADRs; the v1 skill ports are the first issues.

**Consequences.** ✅ The repo is its own integration test and living
example. ❌ Spec maintenance overhead on every design change — which is
the point.
ADR_0012.title: Full dogfood — own spec, starter devcontainer

ADR_0013.content:
**Context.** Two install channels exist with near-identical repo
requirements: the Claude Code plugin marketplace
(``.claude-plugin/plugin.json``) and the skills.sh CLI
(``npx skills add patdhlk/skills``).

**Decision.** Ship both from one structure: ``.claude-plugin/plugin.json``
(plugin name ``patdhlk-skills``) and a flat ``skills/<name>/SKILL.md``
layout. Categories live in the README only.

**Consequences.** ✅ Two channels, near-zero extra cost. ❌ Layout is
constrained by both conventions at once.
ADR_0013.title: Distribution via plugin manifest and skills.sh layout

ADR_0014.content:
**Context.** The mechanized checks beyond the build-time schema —
body lint, verdict checking, next-action queries, duplicate detection
(:need:`ISSUE_0013`–:need:`ISSUE_0018`) — were first sketched as make
targets plus ad-hoc jq. Skills and CI need them deterministic, fast,
and identical across consumer repos. Decided in the grilling session
of 2026-06-06.

**Decision.** A Rust CLI, binary ``pds``, crate ``pds-cli``, living
in this repo at ``cli/`` and versioned in lockstep with the skills.
Scope: build orchestration (shells out to the configured builder),
checks, and queries — verbs ``build``, ``check``, ``lint``,
``verdict-check``, ``next``, ``status``, ``search``, ``dedup``.
Hard invariant: **pds never mutates the spec** — skills author, pds
judges. Output is JSON-only on stdout (logs to stderr); exit codes
are uniform: 0 = clean, 1 = violations found (read the JSON, fix the
corpus), 2 = tool/config error (stop and escalate). Configuration
lives in ``[tool.patdhlk-skills.*]`` sub-tables; an absent table
means the check is off; config referencing undeclared types or
rubrics is a hard error. v1 speaks the local backend only: ``next``
and ``status`` on a github-backend repo exit 2 with the equivalent
``gh`` command, and their JSON shape is versioned so a github driver
can land without skill-text changes. Retrieval for ``search`` and
``dedup`` is BM25 (in-memory, deterministic, offline) by default;
neural embeddings come later behind a cargo feature and
``--engine embed`` with lazy model download. ``dedup`` exits 1 on
hits at/above threshold (a pre-filing gate); ``search`` always
exits 0. Distribution: prebuilt static binaries from GitHub Releases
(``aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin``, ``aarch64/x86_64-linux-musl``) via
an ``install.sh``, with ``cargo install pds-cli`` as fallback;
``/setup-patdhlk-skills`` owns detection and install, as it does for
``ubc``.

**Consequences.** ✅ One deterministic entry point for gates and
queries; agents branch on exit codes instead of parsing prose.
✅ ``make strict`` becomes a thin alias for ``pds check``.
❌ The repo grows a Rust toolchain, CI matrix, and release pipeline.
❌ macOS Gatekeeper: distributed binaries need Developer ID signing
and notarization (or a documented quarantine story) in the release
pipeline.
ADR_0014.title: pds — a Rust gate-and-query CLI

ADR_0015.content:
**Context.** Review judgments (triage verdicts, req-quality reviews)
must be machine-checkable so a gate can require them
(:need:`ISSUE_0014`). JSON receipt files would need a second storage
location, a second read path, and a carve-out from :need:`ADR_0002`.

**Decision.** A verdict is a sphinx-needs directive (new ``verdict``
type, mapped in the role map). One verdict per judged need, linked to
it, with a derived stable ID ``VERDICT_<judged-id>`` — a documented
exception to :need:`ADR_0008`; re-review edits it in place and git
history is the audit trail (:need:`ADR_0005` philosophy). Verdicts
are status-less: staleness is computed, never declared. Fields:
``:rubric:`` (which rubric was judged), ``:axes_failed:`` (failed
axis names; pass is *derived* — true iff empty — so "pass with a
failing axis" is unrepresentable), ``:fingerprint:``
(``sha256:<first-16-hex>`` over the judged need's title + normalized
body; option-field edits like status flips do not invalidate).
Findings are body prose. Verdicts live in ``spec/verdicts/``, are
excluded from default needtables, and are themselves exempt from
lint and from requiring verdicts. ``pds verdict-check`` reports four
buckets: ``missing``, ``failing``, ``stale`` (fingerprint mismatch),
``malformed`` (schema-invalid or unknown axis names).

**Consequences.** ✅ One read path (needs.json) for everything;
verdict coverage is a graph query, visible in the docs. ✅ The
malformed-pass class is impossible by construction. ❌ The corpus
roughly doubles where verdicts are required. ❌ Two ID schemes
coexist (dense max+1 and derived).
ADR_0015.title: Verdicts are needs directives with derived IDs

ADR_0016.content:
**Context.** ``pds`` must reject unknown axis names on verdicts
(:need:`ADR_0015`), but what an axis *means* is judged by the AI
review skills, and consumer repos tailor their type catalogs
(:need:`ADR_0004`) — the binary cannot hardcode this repo's axes.

**Decision.** Rubrics are config:
``[tool.patdhlk-skills.rubrics.<name>] axes = [...]`` declares the
axis set; ``[tool.patdhlk-skills.verdicts] require = { <type> =
"<rubric>" }`` maps need types to required rubrics. ``pds``
validates names and structure only; axis semantics live in the
review skills' prose, where the judge reads them. Default rubric
tables are scaffolded by ``/setup-patdhlk-skills`` — defaults live
in the plugin, not the binary. A ``require`` entry naming an
undeclared rubric is a config hard error (exit 2).

**Consequences.** ✅ The binary stays generic; rubric changes need
no Rust release. ❌ Axis meaning exists only in skill prose —
rubric/skill naming discipline is convention, not enforced.
ADR_0016.title: Rubrics declared in config, semantics in skills

ADR_0017.content:
**Context.** The strict gate (:need:`ADR_0007`) was
``sphinx-build -W -b html`` plus a separate needs.json build — two
builds per mutation, and the gating diagnostics (unknown directives,
broken refs, schema violations, duplicate IDs) come from
parsing/resolution, not HTML rendering.

**Decision.** ``pds check`` runs a per-builder adapter with two
obligations: produce a fresh needs.json and run strict fail-closed
diagnostics. For ``builder = "ubc"``: ``ubc check`` + ``ubc build
needs``. For ``builder = "sphinx-build"``: ``uv run sphinx-build -W
-b needs`` (one build serves both obligations), with a config escape
hatch for projects without ``uv``. The html build is demoted to a
docs-publishing step in CI and is no longer the per-mutation gate.
A divergence (needs-build green, html red) is a ``pds`` bug to fix,
not a reason to gate on html again.

**Consequences.** ✅ One fast build per mutation instead of two.
❌ Rendering-only warnings (toctree gaps, malformed inline markup in
prose) surface only at publish time.
ADR_0017.title: The gate builds needs, not html

FEAT_0001.content:
The skills that read and write the issue store through the configurable
backend (:need:`ADR_0003`): ``/to-prd``, ``/to-issues``, ``/triage``,
``/qa``. These are the reason the GitHub-vs-sphinx-needs backend
abstraction exists.
FEAT_0001.title: Issue-flow core

FEAT_0002.content:
The skills that write durable artifacts — ``arch-decision``, ``req``, and
``term`` directives — into a consumer repo's sphinx-needs spec instead of
markdown ADRs and CONTEXT.md: ``/grill-me``, ``/grill-with-docs``,
``/decide``.
FEAT_0002.title: Docs and decision flow

FEAT_0003.content:
Largely backend-agnostic engineering workflow skills: ``/tdd``,
``/diagnose``, ``/review``, ``/prototype``. They reference the issue store
for context but do not own it.
FEAT_0003.title: Dev-loop skills

FEAT_0004.content:
``/setup-patdhlk-skills`` configures a consumer repo: reads an existing
``ubproject.toml`` and adapts to it (never imposes a catalog), persists
the role map (:need:`ADR_0004`), scaffolds a spec skeleton via uv where
none exists, detects/installs ubc, offers the sphinx-needs-starter
devcontainer, and appends the agent block to CLAUDE.md plus a strict-build
CI workflow.
FEAT_0004.title: Setup and scaffolding

GLOSS_0001.content:
The store a repo's issues live in: ``github`` (GitHub Issues via ``gh``)
or ``sphinx-needs`` (``issue`` directives in the spec). Declared in
``[tool.patdhlk-skills]`` in ``ubproject.toml`` (:need:`ADR_0003`).
Requirements, ADRs, and glossary terms are always sphinx-needs,
regardless of the issue backend.
GLOSS_0001.title: Issue backend

GLOSS_0002.content:
The persisted mapping from abstract skill roles (issue, feature,
requirement, decision, term, test) to a repo's actual directive names,
under ``[tool.patdhlk-skills.roles]``. Skills resolve directives only
through the role map (:need:`ADR_0004`).
GLOSS_0002.title: Role map

GLOSS_0003.content:
The ``sphinx-build -W`` run that ends every skill mutation of the spec.
Schema violations, broken links, and duplicate IDs fail the gate; a
mutation is not done until the gate passes (:need:`ADR_0007`).
GLOSS_0003.title: Strict build gate

GLOSS_0004.content:
The value of an issue's ``:status:`` field, drawn from the state machine
``needs-triage → needs-info | ready-for-agent | ready-for-human →
in-progress → done | wontfix`` (:need:`ADR_0005`). On the github backend
the same names appear as labels.
GLOSS_0004.title: Triage state

GLOSS_0005.content:
The full set of needs in a repo's spec, materialized as ``needs.json``
by ``ubc build needs`` (preferred) or ``sphinx-build -b needs``
(fallback) and queried with jq (:need:`ADR_0006`). Rebuilt before every
query; never cached.
GLOSS_0005.title: Needs corpus

GLOSS_0006.content:
Any engineering artifact meant to outlive a conversation: issues,
requirements, features, architecture decisions, glossary terms, PRDs.
Durable artifacts are sphinx-needs directives in RST, never markdown
(:need:`ADR_0002`).
GLOSS_0006.title: Durable artifact

ISSUE_0001.content:
Build the setup skill. It must: read an existing ``ubproject.toml`` and
propose a role map interactively (persisting it under
``[tool.patdhlk-skills.roles]``); scaffold a spec skeleton via
``uv init`` / ``uv add`` when no sphinx project exists; detect ``ubc`` on
PATH and offer the install script when missing, persisting ``builder``;
offer the sphinx-needs-starter devcontainer (opt-in); append an
``## Agent skills`` block to CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md and offer a strict-build
CI workflow.
ISSUE_0001.title: Implement /setup-patdhlk-skills

ISSUE_0002.content:
Turn conversation context into one RST document per PRD: a single
``feat`` directive with motivation/scope/non-goals prose plus child
``req`` directives linked ``:satisfies:`` the feature (:need:`ADR_0010`).
IDs allocated from a fresh needs.json build (:need:`ADR_0006`,
:need:`ADR_0008`); mutation ends with the strict gate (:need:`ADR_0007`).
ISSUE_0002.title: Implement /to-prd

ISSUE_0003.content:
Slice a PRD's ``req`` directives into independently-grabbable issues.
Backend sphinx-needs: write ``issue`` directives linked to the req IDs.
Backend github: file via ``gh``, with ``Implements: REQ_xxxx`` in the
body and ``:github:`` back-references on the needs (:need:`ADR_0009`).
ISSUE_0003.title: Implement /to-issues

ISSUE_0004.content:
Drive issues through the state machine
``needs-triage → needs-info | ready-for-agent | ready-for-human →
in-progress → done | wontfix`` (:need:`ADR_0005`). Local backend: query
needs.json for ``status == needs-triage``, edit ``:status:`` in place —
git history is the audit trail. GitHub backend: the same states as
labels.
ISSUE_0004.title: Implement /triage

ISSUE_0005.content:
Conversational QA session that files issues from bug reports. Local
backend: append ``issue`` directives (status ``needs-triage``) with
duplicate detection against a fresh needs.json build.
ISSUE_0005.title: Implement /qa

ISSUE_0006.content:
Port unchanged — the skill is backend-agnostic and keeps its name and
behavior (:need:`ADR_0001`).
ISSUE_0006.title: Port /grill-me

ISSUE_0007.content:
Grilling session that challenges a plan against the domain model and
updates documentation inline — but against the sphinx-needs spec:
glossary ``term`` directives instead of CONTEXT.md, ``arch-decision``
directives instead of markdown ADRs (:need:`ADR_0002`).
ISSUE_0007.title: Implement /grill-with-docs

ISSUE_0008.content:
Record a design decision as an ``arch-decision`` directive with Context /
Decision / Consequences structure and links to affected needs. Standalone
implementation — no pharaoh dependency (:need:`ADR_0011`).
ISSUE_0008.title: Implement /decide

ISSUE_0009.content:
Red-green-refactor loop with vertical slices. Touchpoint: resolve issue
references through the backend config when a task cites an ``ISSUE_`` ID
or a GitHub issue number.
ISSUE_0009.title: Port /tdd

ISSUE_0010.content:
Disciplined diagnosis loop (reproduce → minimise → hypothesise →
instrument → fix → regression-test). Backend touchpoint as in
:need:`ISSUE_0009`.
ISSUE_0010.title: Port /diagnose

ISSUE_0011.content:
Review changes since a fixed point along Standards and Spec axes — the
Spec axis reads the originating issue/req from the configured backend
and its linked needs.
ISSUE_0011.title: Port /review

ISSUE_0012.content:
Throwaway prototypes to flesh out a design before committing. Largely
unchanged; findings may feed ``/decide`` (:need:`ISSUE_0008`).
ISSUE_0012.title: Port /prototype

ISSUE_0013.content:
*This was generated by AI during triage.*

The strict gate (:need:`ADR_0007`, :need:`ADR_0017`) catches
structural problems — schema violations, broken links, duplicate
IDs — but says nothing about the substance of a need body.
Implement body lint as a ``pds`` verb (:need:`ADR_0014`), runnable
alone (``pds lint``) and as part of ``pds check``:

- required sections per type (an ``arch-decision`` must carry
  Context / Decision / Consequences; an ``issue`` body must be
  non-trivial),
- weasel-word check on ``req`` bodies (vague adjectives without a
  numeric or behavioral criterion),
- unenumerated-quantifier check on ``req`` bodies (``all`` /
  ``every`` / ``each`` without a stated set),
- optional per-type body-length bounds.

Config in ``[tool.patdhlk-skills.lint]``; absent table = all checks
off, each check individually toggleable. Lint reads a fresh
needs.json (:need:`ADR_0006`), never the RST source. Violations
exit 1 with JSON findings; exempt statuses (:need:`ISSUE_0018`) are
honoured. Depends on the crate scaffold (:need:`ISSUE_0019`).
ISSUE_0013.title: pds lint — mechanized body lint

ISSUE_0014.content:
*This was generated by AI during triage.*

Today a triage or review judgment lives only in the ``:status:``
flip and the git history. Make the judgment itself a gate-checkable
artifact, per :need:`ADR_0015`: register a ``verdict`` need type
(and role-map entry), have review skills author one verdict
directive per judged need — derived ID ``VERDICT_<judged-id>``,
fields ``:rubric:`` / ``:axes_failed:`` / ``:fingerprint:``,
findings as body prose, linked to the judged need, living in
``spec/verdicts/``. Implement ``pds verdict-check``
(:need:`ADR_0014`): derive pass (``axes_failed`` empty), recompute
fingerprints from needs.json, and report four buckets — missing /
failing / stale / malformed — with required types and rubrics read
from ``[tool.patdhlk-skills.verdicts]`` (:need:`ADR_0016`). Wire
into ``pds check --with-verdicts``. The payoff:
``ready-for-agent`` (:need:`ADR_0005`) can require a passing triage
verdict on file, not just an edited status field. Depends on
:need:`ISSUE_0019`.
ISSUE_0014.title: Verdict directives and pds verdict-check

ISSUE_0015.content:
*This was generated by AI during triage.*

An agent driving the backlog should never pick its own work by
grepping around; it should ask the corpus. Implement two ``pds``
verbs (:need:`ADR_0014`): ``pds next`` rebuilds needs.json
(:need:`ADR_0006`) and emits the next actionable issue —
``status == ready-for-agent``, lowest ID first — as JSON (id,
title, status, links); nothing ready is exit 0 with
``{"issue": null, "reason": "none-ready"}``. ``pds status`` emits
per-status counts, giving /triage its "what needs attention"
buckets (:need:`ADR_0005`) from one query path instead of ad-hoc
jq per skill. Both verbs emit a versioned JSON shape; on a
github-backend repo they exit 2 with the equivalent ``gh`` command
(local-only v1, forward-compatible per :need:`ADR_0014`). Depends
on :need:`ISSUE_0019`.
ISSUE_0015.title: pds next and pds status — deterministic backlog queries

ISSUE_0016.content:
*This was generated by AI during triage.*

Retrofit three authoring-discipline patterns into the skills that
loop (/triage, /to-issues, /qa, /tdd, /review):

- **Stopping and escalation section.** Each looping skill
  enumerates exactly which tool outputs mean success, stop, or
  escalate-to-human — so "stuck" is defined by observable state,
  not by the agent's patience.
- **Stage-local validation.** A multi-step authoring loop validates
  only the step just produced; the whole-corpus gate
  (:need:`ADR_0007`) runs once at the end. Running the full gate
  per step stays red until everything exists and causes false
  escalation.
- **Fail-closed judging.** Wherever a skill judges (triage
  recommendation, review verdict): uncertainty is a FAIL with a
  recorded finding, never a silent PASS; a failure with no finding
  is malformed.

The ``pds`` exit contract (:need:`ADR_0014`) is the substrate for
the first two patterns: exit 1 means "read the JSON, fix the
corpus", exit 2 means "stop and escalate" — skills should branch on
that, not on output prose.
ISSUE_0016.title: Stopping contracts and fail-closed judging in looping skills

ISSUE_0017.content:
*This was generated by AI during triage.*

/qa already deduplicates against a fresh needs.json build
(:need:`ISSUE_0005`), but it is the only issue-creating path that
does, and it improvises the matching. Implement the shared engine
as two ``pds`` verbs (:need:`ADR_0014`): ``pds dedup "<candidate>"``
ranks existing needs by similarity and exits 1 when hits reach the
threshold (a pre-filing gate); ``pds search "<query>"`` is the
neutral query form (always exit 0). Both return ranked
``{id, title, score, engine}`` JSON. v1 engine is BM25 — in-memory,
deterministic, offline; neural embeddings come later behind a cargo
feature and ``--engine embed`` with lazy model download, without
changing the JSON contract. Then point every issue-creating path
(/qa, /to-issues, /triage) at ``pds dedup`` before filing. Depends
on :need:`ISSUE_0019`.
ISSUE_0017.title: pds dedup and pds search — retrieval over the corpus

ISSUE_0018.content:
*This was generated by AI during triage.*

Any corpus-wide check added by :need:`ISSUE_0013` or
:need:`ISSUE_0014` must be status-aware: needs whose status is
terminal (``done``, ``wontfix``) are excluded from lint and verdict
requirements before any check runs. Otherwise every new gate
retroactively fails the whole closed backlog and the gates get
disabled instead of trusted. Declare the exempt-status list once in
``ubproject.toml`` under ``[tool.patdhlk-skills.gate]``
(:need:`ADR_0014`) so every ``pds`` check reads the same policy.
ISSUE_0018.title: Status-aware exemption for corpus checks

ISSUE_0019.content:
*This was generated by AI during triage.*

The foundation for :need:`ADR_0014`: a Rust crate at ``cli/``
(crate ``pds-cli``, binary ``pds``), versioned in lockstep with the
skills. Scope of this issue:

- crate skeleton with the verb dispatcher, config loader for
  ``[tool.patdhlk-skills.*]`` (incl. role-map resolution and
  fail-on-drift, exit 2), needs.json reader, and the builder
  adapter (``ubc check`` + ``ubc build needs`` /
  ``uv run sphinx-build -W -b needs`` per :need:`ADR_0017`) —
  enough to ship ``pds build`` and ``pds check`` structural mode;
- JSON-only stdout + 0/1/2 exit contract as shared verb plumbing;
- CI: fmt, clippy, test on the Rust pipeline next to the sphinx
  gate;
- release workflow: static binaries for ``aarch64-apple-darwin``,
  ``x86_64-apple-darwin``, ``x86_64-unknown-linux-musl``,
  ``aarch64-unknown-linux-musl`` to GitHub Releases, an
  ``install.sh``, and ``cargo publish`` of ``pds-cli`` (name
  verified available);
- **macOS Gatekeeper**: downloaded binaries may be blocked when a
  quarantine attribute is set. Decide and implement the story:
  Developer ID codesigning + notarization in the release workflow
  when certificates are available; until then, at minimum ad-hoc
  signing (required on arm64), an ``install.sh`` that clears the
  quarantine attribute on the installed binary with the user's
  consent, and a documented ``cargo install`` fallback (locally
  built binaries are never quarantined).

Windows is deferred (``cargo install`` is the escape hatch). The
other ``pds`` verbs land via :need:`ISSUE_0013` /
:need:`ISSUE_0014` / :need:`ISSUE_0015` / :need:`ISSUE_0017` on top
of this scaffold.
ISSUE_0019.title: Scaffold the pds-cli crate and release pipeline

ISSUE_0020.content:
*This was generated by AI during triage.*

Once :need:`ISSUE_0019` ships, integrate ``pds`` into the system:

- ``/setup-patdhlk-skills`` detects ``pds`` on PATH, offers
  ``install.sh`` when missing (same dance as ``ubc``), and
  scaffolds the default config: ``[tool.patdhlk-skills.gate]``,
  ``.lint``, ``.rubrics.*``, ``.verdicts`` tables
  (:need:`ADR_0016`) plus the ``verdict`` type and role-map entry
  (:need:`ADR_0015`);
- ``make strict`` becomes a thin alias for ``pds check``
  (:need:`ADR_0017`); the html build moves to a docs-publishing CI
  step;
- CLAUDE.md and the affected skill texts switch their gate and
  query instructions to ``pds`` verbs where they exist (ad-hoc
  reads stay jq per :need:`ADR_0006`).
ISSUE_0020.title: Wire pds into setup, gate, and docs

collapse.description: Hide the meta-data information of the need.

content.description: The main content of the need.

github.description: GitHub issue number this need is tracked by (github backend only)

hide.description: If true, the need is not rendered.

kind.description: Issue kind: bug | feature | improvement | chore | question

layout.description: Key of the layout, which is used to render the need.

parts.description: Mapping of parts, a.k.a. sub-needs, IDs to data that overrides the need's data

style.description: Comma-separated list of CSS classes (all appended by `needs_style_`).
